title: macOS screencapture writes Display P3, not sRGB: raw pixel comparison of a screen capture against an engine sRGB buffer manufactures a ~+3 deg hue / +6-8 level R shift on saturated content
suspect: capture tooling only - no engine source implicated; any harness that reads screencapture PNG bytes as if they were sRGB
repro: Boot the web build headful at a pinned 640x480 canvas, wait for GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE=<N> to write /tmp/webgpu_surface_N.ppm, freeze the rAF pump so the presented frame stops advancing, then `screencapture -x -R <canvas rect>` the visible window. Compare the 2x2 box-downsampled capture against the PPM twice: once reading the PNG bytes raw, once after `sips --matchTo '/System/Library/ColorSync/Profiles/sRGB Profile.icc'`. `sips -g profile <capture>.png` reports 'Display P3'. Harness: .superpowers/sdd/task-6-harness/{webprobe6.mjs,img.py,analyze.py}.

evidence.path: .superpowers/sdd/task-6-harness/webprobe6.mjs -- Task 6 comp600 (web dam --deterministic, GPU surface dump frame 600, freeze-verified: two screencaptures 1.2 s apart are byte-identical). Compositor capture vs the engine's own GPU surface buffer, same frame, 2x2 box-downsampled to the buffer's native 640x480. RAW (Display P3 bytes read as sRGB): sky delta [+6.04,+0.93,-1.79] dHue +3.00 deg; skyhi delta [+7.87,+0.36,-2.49] dHue +3.64 deg; but neutral surfaces are unaffected -- rock [+0.28,+0.16,+0.03], ground [+0.41,+0.11,-0.20]. AFTER P3->sRGB conversion: sky delta [+2.18,+1.48,+0.51] dHue +0.05 deg; skyhi delta [+1.52,+1.09,+0.28] dHue -0.07 deg; rock/ground unchanged. The hue error collapses from +3.00/+3.64 deg to +0.05/-0.07 deg. The signature is diagnostic: an sRGB->P3 encode leaves the neutral grey axis untouched (shared white point) and displaces only saturated colours, so a harness that compares neutrals will see nothing wrong while saturated content (sky, blood, muzzle flash) reads systematically hue-shifted.

.superpowers/sdd/task-6-harness/analyze.py -- full write-up in .superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md (section 'Residual (a): compositor path'); sibling of FID-0135 (capture GEOMETRY confound) -- this is the capture COLOUR-SPACE confound.

history.evidence: Task 6 residual-(a) measurement: compositor capture vs GPU surface dump, read both raw-P3 and P3->sRGB-converted. Hue error +3.00/+3.64 deg raw collapses to +0.05/-0.07 deg after conversion; neutral ROIs identical (<=0.41 levels) in both readings. `sips -g profile` confirms the Display P3 profile on every screencapture output.
history.note: Filed by Task 6 while closing the FID-0134 compositor-path residual. This is a measurement trap, not a rendering defect: `screencapture` tags its output with the DISPLAY's colour profile (Display P3 on this Retina Mac), so the stored bytes are P3-encoded. Any harness that reads those bytes and compares them to an engine buffer (which is sRGB) will see a hue shift on saturated content and nothing at all on neutrals -- exactly the shape of a 'the web renders bluer' style false positive. Mitigation for future harnesses: always run `sips --matchTo '/System/Library/ColorSync/Profiles/sRGB Profile.icc'` on a screencapture before pixel comparison, or read and apply the embedded iCCP chunk. Note the residual +1.5 to +2.2 level R/G offset that survives conversion is consistent with the double 8-bit quantisation of the P3->sRGB round trip and is NOT evidence of a render difference. Sibling of FID-0135 (which is the geometry analogue of the same class of confound).
